name: =?utf-8?Q?Eric=20Greitens?=
subject: Must Read Story
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Greitens for Missouri, Jeff Stuerman, Treasurer
body:
Peter,
I hope you'll take a moment to read this story from the Associated Press. Chris Koster is lying about The Mission Continues because he's afraid. He sees his long political career coming to an end, and now he'll say anything to preserve it. Hillary Clinton is even investing $500,000 in Missouri to help spread those lies and implement her agenda through him. But Chris Koster and Hillary Clinton's false attacks on my service to veterans will never change the impact that The Mission Continues has had on peoples' lives. Please read this story and share with all of your friends. Together, we can defeat this kind of politics as usual and take our state in a new direction.

Best,
Eric Greitens

Associated Press (http://www.washingtontimes.com/news/2016/oct/29/marine-missouri-governor-nominee-greitens-saved-my/?utm_source=Master+List&utm_campaign=88091b0d21-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2016_10_29&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_3f71c40094-88091b0d21-99411629) - Marine: Missouri governor nominee Greitens ‘saved my life’
A decade after a brain injury ended his military career, former Marine Paul Joshu had lost his marriage, sobriety and sense of purpose. He was addicted to heroin, contemplating suicide and seemed stuck “in an endless abyss of turmoil.”

That’s when a mentor arranged a meeting with Eric Greitens, a former Navy SEAL officer who founded The Mission Continues charity for veterans. Greitens assured Joshu that he still mattered, that he could be a leader for others and encouraged him to get involved in community service.

“It saved my life, literally,” Joshu said.

Joshu is one of many who credit Greitens with helping to turn around their lives. Now some of those same people are rallying behind Greitens‘ Republican campaign for Missouri governor in the Nov. 8 election against Democratic Attorney General Chris Koster.

Joshu appeared in a TV ad praising Greitens. Other veterans and former The Mission Continues participants have spoken at his campaign rallies. Many who contributed to the charity Greitens founded also are giving money to his first-ever political campaign - an overlap that has raised questions about the extent to which Greitens has tapped into his charitable connections for political gain.

Greitens has cast himself as an outsider ready to blow up politics as usual in Jefferson City. Though Democrats once courted him as a potential candidate, Greitens espouses many traditional Republican positions. He describes himself as anti-abortion, opposes President Barack Obama’s health-care law, supports gun rights and pledges to enact a right-to-work law barring mandating union fees.

But Greitens‘ campaign has been built largely upon a theme of personal leadership displayed through his military, humanitarian and charitable service.

“Leadership,” Greitens said in an interview with The Associated Press, “for the purpose of helping people to change and save their lives.”

At age 42, Greitens already has been a Rhodes scholar and White House fellow, a champion boxer and marathon winner, a best-selling author and motivational speaker. He’s been awarded the Bronze Star, Purple Heart and Navy Reserve Junior Line Officer of the Year. He’s a member of the Missouri Public Affairs Hall of Fame. And he has been ranked by magazines among the world’s most influential people because of his work at The Mission Continues.
Those who know him say Greitens has always been ambitious.

His mother was an early childhood special education teacher and his father an accountant for the U.S. Department of Agriculture. But family friends from suburban St. Louis have said Greitens began talking in elementary school already about wanting to become president. His heroes in kindergarten included President Theodore Roosevelt and British Prime Minister Winston Churchill.

“I was very young when I first started thinking about how I was going to live a good life,” Greitens said.

Some professors at Duke University also foresaw Greitens as a future leader. While other students partied, Greitens trained as boxer, studied in China and traveled to Croatia, Rwanda and Bolivia to document the conditions of children separated from their parents by war and poverty.

“Eric was almost like a classic throwback to kind of an era where developing a solid character and informed world view was actually an important part of your education,” said former Duke public policy professor Neil Boothby, who now is at Columbia University.

After earning a doctoral degree in politics from Oxford University in England, Greitens says he spurned lucrative job offers to become a Navy SEAL officer. He was injured while deployed in Iraq and, when he returned home, was inspired by visiting other wounded servicemen to help provide them a renewed sense of purpose through community service.

As he was contemplating how to do this, Greitens met with various people, including Wayne Fields, then the director of the American Cultural Studies Program at Washington University in St. Louis. Fields got the impression Greitens “was putting together a resume.”

“I think it was genuinely an effort to try to find something good to do, but I think it was also clearly from the start designed to create a platform for a political career,” said Fields, an English professor whose expertise is in political rhetoric.

After founding The Mission Continues, Greitens also launched a career as an author and motivational speaker. It’s unclear how much he’s earned from that because he has declined to release his tax returns. Greitens has fashioned himself as warrior-philosopher, a leader in the mold of ancient Greek heroes. He encourages others to similarly chart a course for who they want to be, then set out to achieve it.

“Eric has a talent in offering purpose, direction and motivation for people,” said Mike Pereira, a former Army officer who met Greitens in Iraq.

Years later, Pereira followed Greitens‘ model of community service to overcome his post-military depression and worked for a while at The Mission Continues. It was Pereira who introduced Greitens to Joshu, the former Marine struggling with life.

Joshu said Greitens took time to listen and care and encouraged him to set goals. It started small - making his bed every day - and progressed to volunteering at nonprofits and enrolling in college.

“It really gave me a new perspective on life and what I could do and how I could change myself using the skills I had from the Marines Corps,” Joshu said.

Paid for by Greitens for Missouri, Jeff Stuerman, Treasurer

4579 Laclede Avenue #138
St. Louis, Missouri 63108

Eric is a former member of the United States Navy having served four deployments overseas. Use of his military rank, titles and photographs in uniform does not imply an endorsement from the Department of the Navy or the Department of Defense.

This email was sent to dpwillis67+greitens@gmail.com. You can also follow us on Facebook (https://facebook.com/ericgreitens?utm_source=Master+List&utm_campaign=88091b0d21-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2016_10_29&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_3f71c40094-88091b0d21-99411629) and Twitter (https://twitter.com/ericgreitens?utm_source=Master+List&utm_campaign=88091b0d21-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2016_10_29&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_3f71c40094-88091b0d21-99411629) . Emails are one of the best ways to stay connected with the campaign. If they become too much, you can unsubscribe (http://ericgreitens.us13.list-manage1.com/unsubscribe?u=c81357bdb59fb170d9eb82b3a&id=3f71c40094&e=84041254e6&c=88091b0d21) , but please let us know how we can improve. You can always reach us at team@ericgreitens.com (mailto:team@ericgreitens.com) or (314) 899-0288. You're still reading the fineprint? You must be a detail oriented, all-around awesome person who is a big supporter — you should volunteer (http://ericgreitens.com/volunteer?utm_source=Master+List&utm_campaign=88091b0d21-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2016_10_29&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_3f71c40094-88091b0d21-99411629) with us!
clean_body: Peter, I hope you'll take a moment to read this story from the Associated Press. Chris Koster is lying about The Mission Continues because he's afraid. He sees his long political career coming to an end, and now he'll say anything to preserve it. Hillary Clinton is even investing $500,000 in Missouri to help spread those lies and implement her agenda through him. But Chris Koster and Hillary Clinton's false attacks on my service to veterans will never change the impact that The Mission Continues has had on peoples' lives. Please read this story and share with all of your friends. Together, we can defeat this kind of politics as usual and take our state in a new direction. Best, Eric Greitens Associated Press () - Marine: Missouri governor nominee Greitens ‘saved my life’ A decade after a brain injury ended his military career, former Marine Paul Joshu had lost his marriage, sobriety and sense of purpose. He was addicted to heroin, contemplating suicide and seemed stuck “in an endless abyss of turmoil.” That’s when a mentor arranged a meeting with Eric Greitens, a former Navy SEAL officer who founded The Mission Continues charity for veterans. Greitens assured Joshu that he still mattered, that he could be a leader for others and encouraged him to get involved in community service. “It saved my life, literally,” Joshu said. Joshu is one of many who credit Greitens with helping to turn around their lives. Now some of those same people are rallying behind Greitens‘ Republican campaign for Missouri governor in the Nov. 8 election against Democratic Attorney General Chris Koster. Joshu appeared in a TV ad praising Greitens. Other veterans and former The Mission Continues participants have spoken at his campaign rallies. Many who contributed to the charity Greitens founded also are giving money to his first-ever political campaign - an overlap that has raised questions about the extent to which Greitens has tapped into his charitable connections for political gain. Greitens has cast himself as an outsider ready to blow up politics as usual in Jefferson City. Though Democrats once courted him as a potential candidate, Greitens espouses many traditional Republican positions. He describes himself as anti-abortion, opposes President Barack Obama’s health-care law, supports gun rights and pledges to enact a right-to-work law barring mandating union fees. But Greitens‘ campaign has been built largely upon a theme of personal leadership displayed through his military, humanitarian and charitable service. “Leadership,” Greitens said in an interview with The Associated Press, “for the purpose of helping people to change and save their lives.” At age 42, Greitens already has been a Rhodes scholar and White House fellow, a champion boxer and marathon winner, a best-selling author and motivational speaker. He’s been awarded the Bronze Star, Purple Heart and Navy Reserve Junior Line Officer of the Year. He’s a member of the Missouri Public Affairs Hall of Fame. And he has been ranked by magazines among the world’s most influential people because of his work at The Mission Continues. Those who know him say Greitens has always been ambitious. His mother was an early childhood special education teacher and his father an accountant for the U.S. Department of Agriculture. But family friends from suburban St. Louis have said Greitens began talking in elementary school already about wanting to become president. His heroes in kindergarten included President Theodore Roosevelt and British Prime Minister Winston Churchill. “I was very young when I first started thinking about how I was going to live a good life,” Greitens said. Some professors at Duke University also foresaw Greitens as a future leader. While other students partied, Greitens trained as boxer, studied in China and traveled to Croatia, Rwanda and Bolivia to document the conditions of children separated from their parents by war and poverty. “Eric was almost like a classic throwback to kind of an era where developing a solid character and informed world view was actually an important part of your education,” said former Duke public policy professor Neil Boothby, who now is at Columbia University. After earning a doctoral degree in politics from Oxford University in England, Greitens says he spurned lucrative job offers to become a Navy SEAL officer. He was injured while deployed in Iraq and, when he returned home, was inspired by visiting other wounded servicemen to help provide them a renewed sense of purpose through community service. As he was contemplating how to do this, Greitens met with various people, including Wayne Fields, then the director of the American Cultural Studies Program at Washington University in St. Louis. Fields got the impression Greitens “was putting together a resume.” “I think it was genuinely an effort to try to find something good to do, but I think it was also clearly from the start designed to create a platform for a political career,” said Fields, an English professor whose expertise is in political rhetoric. After founding The Mission Continues, Greitens also launched a career as an author and motivational speaker. It’s unclear how much he’s earned from that because he has declined to release his tax returns. Greitens has fashioned himself as warrior-philosopher, a leader in the mold of ancient Greek heroes. He encourages others to similarly chart a course for who they want to be, then set out to achieve it. “Eric has a talent in offering purpose, direction and motivation for people,” said Mike Pereira, a former Army officer who met Greitens in Iraq. Years later, Pereira followed Greitens‘ model of community service to overcome his post-military depression and worked for a while at The Mission Continues. It was Pereira who introduced Greitens to Joshu, the former Marine struggling with life. Joshu said Greitens took time to listen and care and encouraged him to set goals. It started small - making his bed every day - and progressed to volunteering at nonprofits and enrolling in college. “It really gave me a new perspective on life and what I could do and how I could change myself using the skills I had from the Marines Corps,” Joshu said. Paid for by Greitens for Missouri, Jeff Stuerman, Treasurer 4579 Laclede Avenue #138 St. Louis, Missouri 63108 Eric is a former member of the United States Navy having served four deployments overseas. Use of his military rank, titles and photographs in uniform does not imply an endorsement from the Department of the Navy or the Department of Defense.

Emails are a critical way for us to stay in touch with our grassroots supporters. 99% of the DSCC's contributions come from grassroots donors giving less than $200. We also strive to keep you updated on issues related to the Democratic Party's values. If you'd like to take a break from DSCC emails, "". If you'd like to make a contribution to support us, please "". Together, we're going to take back the Senate in November, but we'll need the help of every single grassroots supporter to do it. If you'd like to stop receiving all emails from the DSCC, please "". We greatly appreciate your continued support. Contributions or gifts to the DSCC are not tax deductible.

body:
Senator Johnson is taking the low road in the last days of this campaign. | [](http://action.russfeingold.com/page/m/66cf4641/1c469400/1c52df0/35428de/3464797593/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/oct-eom?utm_source=ema_2016-10-29_eom_8-nd&refcode=ema_2016-10-29_eom_8-nd&utm_campaign=eom&utm_medium=ema") Friend, There's just over a week until Election Day, and something has happened to Senator Ron Johnson. Any semblance of "Wisconsin nice" is gone: He's resorting to nasty, personal attacks on Russ. This is exactly why it's so important that we reach our final end-of-month goal by midnight on Monday. Senator Johnson's reported to have $600,000 more cash-on-hand than us. And billionaire Diane Hendricks just gave Senator Johnson's super PAC $5 million dollars of last-minute cash. We have to close the gap. [**Donate $5 today to help Russ reach his final end-of-month goal of this campaign.**](http://action.russfeingold.com/page/m/66cf4641/1c469400/1c52df0/35428de/3464797593/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/oct-eom?utm_source=ema_2016-10-29_eom_8-nd&refcode=ema_2016-10-29_eom_8-nd&utm_campaign=eom&utm_medium=ema") [](http://action.russfeingold.com/page/m/66cf4641/1c469400/1c52df0/35428de/3464797593/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/oct-eom?utm_source=ema_2016-10-29_eom_8-nd&refcode=ema_2016-10-29_eom_8-nd&utm_campaign=eom&utm_medium=ema") [](http://action.russfeingold.com/page/m/66cf4641/1c469400/1c52df0/35428de/3464797593/VEsC/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/oct-eom?utm_source=ema_2016-10-29_eom_8-nd&refcode=ema_2016-10-29_eom_8-nd&utm_campaign=eom&utm_medium=ema") _Or, if your payment information is saved with ActBlue, click below and your contribution will go through immediately:_ | | [$5 »](http://action.russfeingold.com/page/m/66cf4641/1c469400/1c52df0/35428df/3464797593/VEsD/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/oct-eom?utm_source=ema_2016-10-29_eom_8-nd&refcode=ema_2016-10-29_eom_8-nd&utm_campaign=eom&utm_medium=ema&express_lane=true&amount=5")

Senator Johnson told the _Milwaukee_ _Journal Sentinel_ that he doesn't respect Russ, repeatedly calling Russ a "phony." Classy. Senator Johnson's extra $600,000 could go toward anything he wants -- like more misleading ads attacking Russ to try and scoop up undecided voters.
Senator Johnson's choosing to take the low road in the last few days of this campaign. But his personal attacks won't work, because this is exactly what people hate about partisan politics. And we're going to stay on track. We're going to work harder than ever to elect Russ.
[**We're one day away from closing the books on our final end-of-month goal, and we still need your help: Can you donate $5 right now to help us get there?**](http://action.russfeingold.com/page/m/66cf4641/1c469400/1c52df0/35428de/3464797593/VEsP/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/oct-eom?utm_source=ema_2016-10-29_eom_8-nd&refcode=ema_2016-10-29_eom_8-nd&utm_campaign=eom&utm_medium=ema")
With your support, we'll make more calls, knock more doors, and turn out more voters to elect Democrats up and down the ballot on November 8 -- and send Senator Johnson and his angry insults packing.
Thanks,
Tom Russell
Campaign Manager

We can't waste this chance: With only 10 days to go in New Hampshire's race that will decide Senate control and the future of the Supreme Court, **a group of donors has agreed to EXTEND the match -- but only if we hit our new $27,700 goal.**
This could not come at a more critical time, Friends. Thanks to over $50 MILLION in spending from Ayotte's corporate special interest backers, a recent poll shows Governor Hassan is THREE POINTS DOWN.

name: John Podesta, HillaryClinton.com
subject: A note re: Comey's letter and what’s next
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Hillary for America, a grassroots campaign of 2
body:
Here's what's really going on | | Derek --

By now, you've probably heard some news about a bewildering letter sent to Congressional leaders by the Director of the FBI.

This is all a bit confusing, and much of the reporting has been misleading or downright inaccurate, so I want to take a minute to walk through this. Bear with me!

Here's the quick backstory: In July, FBI Director James Comey concluded a year-long investigation by deciding not to go forward with any case about Hillary's emails. It wasn't even a close call, he said. In his words, "no reasonable prosecutor" would have brought charges.

Yesterday, in a surprise to us all, Comey wrote a very strange letter to Congress that was long on innuendo and short on facts. He said that in a completely separate investigation, the FBI had found some emails that may or may not be related to Hillary, and indeed may or may not be significant at all.

It's since been reported that these emails may not have even been sent by or to Hillary; that they weren't withheld by Hillary or the campaign in the earlier investigation; and most or even all of them may be duplicates of emails already in the FBI's possession.

It's being reported that Comey sent this letter over the objections of Department of Justice officials who told him that it was inconsistent with longstanding policy of both Democratic and Republican administrations not to take action that might impact an election. It's an unprecedented intrusion into a close presidential election with 10 days until Election Day.

But by being vague and obfuscating, Comey opened the door to conspiracy theories, Republican attacks against Hillary, and a surge of fundraising for Trump and his team. So this bears repeating: **There is no evidence of wrongdoing, no charge of wrongdoing, and no indication that any of this even involves Hillary.**

Voters deserve answers.

Comey needs to come clean with the American people about what he found and answer all the questions about why he took this unprecedented step less than two weeks before the election.

**Here's what this changes for you and this campaign: Absolutely nothing.**

Trump has spent more than a year trying to bully his way to the presidency. He's been browbeating the FBI (indeed, many have speculated that Comey took this extraordinary action in part to reduce the pressure on his agency that Trump's campaign and his Republican Congressional allies has been building up), leading crowds in chants of "lock her up," and even saying Hillary should be in prison -- and if he won, he'd put her there.

The facts be damned -- Trump's always shown a complete disregard for the truth. In spite of there being no new information, he's already raising money off the bizarre letter (he literally sent a text message asking for money within an hour of the story breaking!) and he's more fired up than ever on the trail today.

Derek, these are the actions of a desperate man. Trump knows he can't win on his character, temperament, or his policies. He's behind, so he'll cling to whatever he can.

So, no, our strategy doesn't change. But Derek, our intensity needs to, because we can't let him get away with this.

Our organizers and volunteers on the ground are rallying behind Hillary in what will be the biggest volunteer weekend of the campaign so far. You need to get her back, too. You need to say you're not willing to let Trump bully or buy his way into the presidency, and you're not going to let anything stop us from making history.

**There are ten days to go. Let's buckle down, stay focused, and win this.** | **You’re one of our most committed supporters. If you've saved your payment information, your donation will go through immediately:
** | **[**Derek, donate now: $1**](https://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=3&ms=MjY5MjUzNTMS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=OTA1MjQ1NjM4S0&mt=1&rt=0) **
---

**[**Derek, donate now: $10**](https://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=10&ms=MjY5MjUzNTMS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=OTA1MjQ1NjM4S0&mt=1&rt=0)**
**[**Derek, donate now: $25**](https://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=11&ms=MjY5MjUzNTMS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=OTA1MjQ1NjM4S0&mt=1&rt=0)**
**[**Derek, donate now: $50**](https://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=17&ms=MjY5MjUzNTMS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=OTA1MjQ1NjM4S0&mt=1&rt=0)**
**
Thanks,

John

John Podesta
Chair
Hillary for America

Thanks so much for doing everything you can to elect Hillary Clinton as our next president. Hillary for America, P.O. Box 5256, New York, NY 10185 [Privacy Policy](https://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=5&ms=MjY5MjUzNTMS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=OTA1MjQ1NjM4S0&mt=1&rt=0)

name: Tracy Newman
subject: FWD: re: Closing Deadline

body:
Did you see Jay's email? | [](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=2&ms=OTc4MjcwMQS2&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTAyMjMzNTI2MwS2&mt=1&rt=0)
Dear Friend, I want to make sure Jay's email is at the top of your inbox. It couldn't be more important to keep working nonstop to elect Jay until Election Day -- that means ads, that means GOTV field efforts, and yes, that means resources. This is Jay's last end-of-month deadline, and make no mistake -- it's his most important deadline. [**Can you help with a donation before midnight Monday?**](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=5&ms=OTc4MjcwMQS2&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTAyMjMzNTI2MwS2&mt=1&rt=0) Thanks for standing with Jay, Tracy Newman
Finance Director \---------- Forwarded message ----------
From: Jay Inslee
Date: Saturday, Oct 29, 2016
Subject: re: Closing Deadline Friend, We've reached the last end-of-month deadline for our campaign before Election Day. With the end of October, we're now in the homestretch of this long election. And that's why I'm asking for your help. What we raise by midnight on Monday powers our campaign through these final days. We're not leaving anything on the table as we head into November 8th. Whether it's strengthening our Get Out the Vote operation or keeping our television ads on the air, we've gotta go all out - because this year, too much is at stake. There's still work to be done in Washington. Going backward, as my opponent proposes, is not an option.
Growing our economy, protecting the environment, providing educational opportunities for every student, and leveling the economic playing field for hardworking Washingtonians -- these are some of our progressive priorities for the next four years.
Voters are deciding right now -- and will continue making their choice for governor until Election Night.
**Will you step up and help as we make our final push?**
Very truly yours,
Jay Inslee
